page: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.6xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8252C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The instance provides 24 dedicated vCPUs across 12 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. No local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators are included in this configuration.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and a cached small-block read bandwidth of 1645.5 GB/sec. CPU performance is generally average, with a PassMark CPU score of 28155.2 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11173.0. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength, reaching 31.88 GB/sec. However, the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration limits its utility for large-scale AI workloads; while small 135M LLM inference runs at average speeds, prompt processing for a large 70B model drops to a poor 4.04 tokens/sec.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfer for distributed environments. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density of 768.0 GB relative to 24 vCPUs positions this instance as a specialized resource for memory-bound workloads rather than general compute. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which should be factored into architectural planning. This profile is suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.6xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, driven by an Intel Xeon 8252C processor at 4.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Benchmark results show strong memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and a cached small-block read bandwidth of 1645.5 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 31.88 GB/sec, while general CPU benchmarks fall in the average range. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, making it inefficient for large LLM inference, where 70B model prompt processing drops to 4.04 tokens/sec. With a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as large in-memory databases, Redis caching, and enterprise application hosting.
